title: A War Far Away Makes Things More Expensive for a Family
summary: A war between the US, Israel, and Iran started on February 28. This war is far away from the UK, but it is making things more expensive for families there. Fuel prices have gone up a lot. For example, filling a car with petrol costs about £14 more, and diesel costs about £27 more. Naomi is a mum who lives in Chorley, Lancashire. She has a daughter named Riziah who is 10 years old and has serious health problems. Riziah needs to go to the hospital in Liverpool often, which is more than 30 miles away. The family uses a van for these trips. Now, filling the van costs almost £130, which is £30 more than before. Naomi is also worried about her energy bills going up in July. Riziah needs extra electricity for her medical devices, and the house must be kept warm all year. The family is trying to cut costs, but it is hard. Naomi does not want her children to see how worried she is.

background_read: This article talks about how a war between the US, Israel, and Iran affects a family in the UK. The war started on February 28. Even though it is far away, it makes fuel and energy more expensive. Families like Naomi's have to spend more money on petrol and electricity. Naomi's daughter Riziah has health problems and needs to go to the hospital often. The family also needs to keep the house warm for her. The UK government has a price cap to help with energy bills, but prices are still expected to go up.
Article_Structure: WHO: Naomi, her husband, and their two children. Riziah is 10 and has medical conditions. They live in Chorley, Lancashire.
WHAT: The war between the US, Israel, and Iran has caused fuel prices to rise. This makes it harder for the family to afford trips to the hospital and energy bills.
WHERE: The war is in the Middle East, but the family lives in the UK. They travel to Liverpool for hospital visits.
WHY: The war started on February 28, which led to higher fuel prices. The family has no choice but to travel for medical care, so they are spending more money.

perspectives.description: The family is worried about money and does not want the children to see their stress.
The war has caused fuel prices to rise by 19-35%, affecting many families.
